---
title: "MediaPoint Widget"
format: html
---

## Overview

`MediaPoint_Widget` is the sub-widget within `Media_Widget` responsible for
configuring and interacting with point data. It provides:

1. **Style controls**: Shape, size, color, and alpha for point markers
2. **Point interaction**: Select, move, and add points via mouse clicks with modifier keys

## Style Controls (GlyphStyleControls)

Point visual properties are managed through an embedded `GlyphStyleControls` widget
backed by a `GlyphStyleState` instance. This replaces the previous bespoke
`ColorPicker_Widget`, size slider/spinbox, and marker shape combo box with the
reusable glyph style infrastructure from `Plots/Common/GlyphStyleWidget/`.

### Supported Glyph Types

The marker shapes come from `CorePlotting::GlyphType`:

| GlyphType | Rendering |
|-----------|-----------|
| Circle    | Filled ellipse |
| Square    | Filled rectangle |
| Tick      | Vertical line |
| Cross     | '+' shaped marker |
| Diamond   | Rotated square |
| Triangle  | Filled triangle |

### Data Flow

```
GlyphStyleControls (UI)
       ↕ signals
GlyphStyleState (QObject wrapper)
       ↕ sync methods
PointDisplayOptions (serializable struct)
       ↕ stored in
MediaWidgetState::DisplayOptionsRegistry
       ↕ read by
Media_Window::_plotPointData() (rendering)
```

- `_syncGlyphStateFromOptions()`: Loads `PointDisplayOptions` into `GlyphStyleState` when
  the active key changes (uses `setStyleSilent()` to avoid triggering a render loop)
- `_applyGlyphStateToOptions()`: Writes `GlyphStyleState` changes back to
  `PointDisplayOptions` and triggers a canvas update

## Point Interaction

Mouse interaction uses modifier keys:

| Action | Modifier | Behavior |
|--------|----------|----------|
| Select point | Click | Finds nearest point within threshold, highlights in yellow |
| Move point | Ctrl+Click | Moves selected point to click position |
| Add point | Alt+Click | Adds new point at click position at current time |

## Display Options

`PointDisplayOptions` stores per-key styling in `MediaWidgetStateData::point_options`.
The struct contains:

- `point_size` (int): Marker size in pixels
- `marker_shape` (`CorePlotting::GlyphType`): Marker shape enum
- Common fields via `rfl::Flatten<CommonDisplayFields>`: `hex_color`, `alpha`, `is_visible`
